ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic burrito class; no prawn-specific burrito entry exists. Mapped to closest parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers burrito as a composite dish; no prawn-specific variant code exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for prawn burrito or seafood burrito as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard prawn burrito weighing approximately 220 g, as typically served in a casual dining or food-court setting in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Prawn Burritos — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Prawn Burritos (cr_id G00010822). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (shrimp burrito, SR Legacy) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on wheat flour tortilla wrap with mixed filling
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for prawn burritos. GI estimated at 58 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle being a wheat flour tortilla (GI ~50–60 per University of Sydney GI Database, entry for wheat flour tortilla / soft taco shell) combined with white rice filling (GI ~72) and protein/fat dampening effect from prawns, cheese, and sour cream. Weighted composite estimate yields GI ~58. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × net carbs per serving) / 100 = (58 × 40.5) / 100 ≈ 23.5 for a 220 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 470 mg/100 g with low potassium offset (126 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit prawn burrito consumption to small portions due to the high sodium content of 470 mg/100 g; the low potassium-to-sodium ratio (0.27) provides insufficient cardiovascular offset, and a 220 g serving delivers approximately 1034 mg sodium, representing nearly half the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate-GI carbohydrates (20.1 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (1.4 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume prawn burritos cautiously; the estimated GI of 58 and low fibre content of 1.4 g/100 g (below the 3 g/100 g threshold) produce a glycemic load of approximately 23.5 per serving, which may cause a moderate postprandial glucose rise — substituting white rice with cauliflower rice and increasing bean content can meaningfully reduce glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated cholesterol (212 mg/100 g) and estimated saturated fat (~5.5 g/100 g) from cheese and sour cream
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the high cholesterol content of 212 mg/100 g and estimated saturated fat of approximately 5.5 g/100 g from cheese and sour cream are clinically relevant; a 220 g serving provides roughly 466 mg dietary cholesterol, approaching the upper limit recommended for at-risk individuals, and reducing cheese and sour cream while increasing prawn proportion would improve the saturated-to-unsaturated fat ratio.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~5.5 g/100 g from cheese and sour cream without sufficient omega-3 anti-inflammatory offset
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: For individuals with rheumatoid arthritis, the estimated saturated fat of ~5.5 g/100 g may promote pro-inflammatory cytokine activity (TNF-alpha, IL-6); reducing cheese and sour cream and increasing the prawn proportion would leverage the omega-3 content of prawns to partially offset this inflammatory burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Prawn Burritos are a Western (Mexican-inspired) composite dish adapted for the Singapore food market, consisting of succulent cooked prawns (shrimp) wrapped in a soft wheat flour tortilla together with white rice, cheese, sour cream, beans, salsa, and seasoning. The dish delivers a rich, savoury flavour profile with a creamy, slightly tangy finish from the sour cream and cheese, contrasted by the firm texture of the prawns and the soft chew of the tortilla. Per 100 g, the dish provides 249 kcal, 10.91 g protein, 13.71 g fat, and 20.06 g carbohydrates, with a notable sodium content of 470 mg and cholesterol of 212 mg, reflecting the combined contribution of prawns, cheese, and seasoning. Dietary fibre is low at 1.4 g/100 g. The estimated glycemic index is 58 (medium), driven primarily by the wheat flour tortilla and white rice filling, with protein and fat from prawns and dairy providing partial glycemic dampening. Clinically, the dish is a moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to its moderate GI and low fibre, a high concern for hypertension due to elevated sodium with poor potassium offset, and a moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to elevated cholesterol and saturated fat from dairy components. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with crustacean or gluten allergies. It is typically consumed as a lunch or dinner item in casual dining settings in Singapore. In Chinese it is known as 虾卷饼, in Malay as Burrito Udang, in Tamil as இறால் புரிட்டோ, and in Hindi as झींगा बुरिटो.
common_user_queries: How many calories are in a prawn burrito?
Is prawn burrito high in sodium?
What is the GI of a prawn burrito?
Is prawn burrito suitable for hypertension?
How much protein is in a prawn burrito?
Is shrimp burrito the same as prawn burrito?
What is prawn burrito called in Chinese?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id G00010822, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat is estimated from a USDA SR Legacy analogue (shrimp burrito composite) and documented accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using the wheat flour tortilla as the primary carbohydrate vehicle. Multilingual names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are standard transliterations/translations of the dish name appropriate for the Singapore market context.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (5.5 g/100 g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy shrimp burrito composite analogy, as the HPB Foodo entry does not separately report saturated fat for this dish. This value is used for clinical guidance only and should be replaced with a direct measurement when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, fat-soluble vitamins, B-vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this specific prawn burrito preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI of 58 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for prawn burritos exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 4. Halal status is not confirmed; prawns are halal but cheese and sour cream sourcing requires verification. 5. This dish is classified under food_group 'Fish and seafood' per the HPB source record, though it is a composite dish with significant carbohydrate and dairy components. 6. Multilingual names (Chinese: 虾卷饼 / 蝦卷餅, Malay: Burrito Udang, Tamil: இறால் புரிட்டோ, Hindi: झींगा बुरिटो) are provided as the dish is listed in the Singapore HPB database and consumed in the Singapore market context, which requires multilingual coverage per G6 rules. These are standard descriptive translations of the dish name.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is 'Prawn Burritos', a precise canonical English name. The dish is listed in the Singapore HPB database and consumed in the Singapore market; accordingly, mandatory local-language names are provided: Chinese (虾卷饼 / 蝦卷餅), Malay (Burrito Udang), Tamil (இறால் புரிட்டோ), and Hindi (झींगா बुरिटो). G6 multilingual compliance is fully satisfied.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (249.0), protein_g (10.91), fat_g (13.71), and carbohydrate_g (20.06) are all present, numeric, and sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo canonical entry G00010822 (confidence 0.95). nutrient_source is fully populated.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.4), sodium_mg (470.0), saturated_fat_g (5.5, estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogue and documented in notes), and sugar_g (1.75) are all present as numeric values and plausible for this dish type.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (58), gi_category ('medium'), glycemic_load_per_serving (23.5), and gi_source are all present. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using wheat flour tortilla as the primary carbohydrate vehicle; estimation basis is fully documented in gi_source.source_match_notes.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI mapped to burrito (food product) class (closest_parent match, gap noted). FoodEx2 code A0CKP for burrito is a defensible closest_parent match (gap noted). SNOMED CT is null with gap note. No codes are invented; all are honest approximate mappings with documented gaps.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters, covers what the food is, preparation method, taste and texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, key nutrients, and multilingual identity.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions are present: Hypertension (high concern), Type 2 Diabetes (moderate concern, human_condition_name correctly set to 'Type 2 Diabetes'), and Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and a specific clinical guidance sentence. Rheumatoid Arthritis is included as an additional condition.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: Flat aliases array contains all strings from aliases_by_language: English variants (Prawn Burritos, Shrimp Burritos, Prawn Burrito, Shrimp Burrito, Seafood Burrito), Chinese (虾卷饼, 蝦卷餅), Malay (Burrito Udang), Tamil (இறால் புரிட்டோ), Hindi (झींगा बुरिटो). Self-check performed: every string in aliases_by_language appears verbatim in the flat aliases array. Mandatory language coverage for Singapore market context is complete.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' for FoodOn and FoodEx2.

Iteration 3: Added mandatory multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 and Q1 requirements for Singapore HPB-sourced dish. Added corresponding entries to aliases_by_language and flat aliases array. Verified aliases consistency: all aliases_by_language strings appear verbatim in flat aliases array (Q8 self-check passed). Updated notes to document multilingual name rationale. Updated confidence_reason and llm_training.natural_language_description to reference multilingual names.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Prawn Burritos (G00010822) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard. Iteration 3 resolved the G6 multilingual naming failure by adding Chinese (虾卷饼 / 蝦卷餅), Malay (Burrito Udang), Tamil (இறால் புரிட்டோ), and Hindi (झींगा बुरिटो) local names and aliases, as required for a dish listed in the Singapore HPB database. All aliases_by_language entries are consistent with the flat aliases array. All other sections remain robust from previous iterations.
